summary: First-screen Court Brief and compressed guidance largely satisfy the orientation goal, but default player mode still exposes developer/debug/internal copy. This fails the explicit player-mode no-debug-leak acceptance criterion and needs a small UI/i18n/test correction before gameplay acceptance.

tests_run.result: PASS; branch was clean at start of review.
PASS; reviewed changed UI, i18n, tests, Storybook, task, route, and message files.

tests_run.command: manual inspection of packages/ui/src/index.tsx, packages/ui/src/i18n.ts, tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts, tests/ui-m2-district-list.test.tsx
tests_run.result: REQUEST_CHANGES; player-mode developer/debug/internal copy remains visible and tests assert one instance.

decisions: Court Brief placement and content are acceptable in principle: it answers identity, date/season, priority problem, recommended action, cost, and ignored consequence without opening another panel.
Generic guidance is improved from a checklist into Objective/Focus/Action/Safety and keeps preview/confirmation safety.
No scope drift found into sim/domain rules, protocol, save format, full inspector rewrite, task rail, final art, or manual battle.
risks: Leaving developer-overlay and diagnostics text on the default first screen keeps the UI feeling like a tool/debug build rather than a player-facing game screen.
Current E2E coverage misses the acceptance wording because it checks for state hash/prototype/raw reason leaks but not developer/debug/internal player-mode labels.
blockers: Remove or hide player-mode Developer Overlay / developer diagnostics copy from the default player screen, or replace it with player-facing neutral status text.
Update E2E/SSR assertions so player mode fails on Developer Overlay/developer diagnostics/internal debug text while preserving preview/confirmation safety coverage.
requested_action: Route back to client_engineer for the minimal player-mode debug/internal copy cleanup and matching test update, then return to gameplay_designer review.
